title: Chapter 1: Greetings & Basics
description: Learn to say hello, goodbye, and basic introductions.

title: Chapter 2: Locations
description: Express where things and people are using 'estar'.

sentences.english: Yes, I am at home now.

sentences.spanish: No, no estoy en casa ahora.
sentences.english: No, I am not at home now.

sentences.spanish: Sí, estoy en el trabajo hoy.
sentences.english: Yes, I am at work today.

sentences.spanish: No, no estoy en el trabajo hoy.
sentences.english: No, I am not at work today.
Yes, the school is far away.
We are friends and we are here.

sentences.spanish: Soy Ana y estoy en casa.
sentences.english: I am Ana and I am at home.

sentences.spanish: Hoy no estoy en la escuela.
sentences.english: Today I am not at school.

sentences.spanish: Sí, Luis está en el café.
sentences.english: Yes, Luis is in the café.
Are Marta and Carlos at home?

title: Chapter 3: Descriptions
description: Describe objects and people using 'ser' and adjectives.

sentences.spanish: Un café está en el centro.

sentences.english: Is it a book or a table?
Is it a house or a café?

title: Chapter 4: Possession
description: Talk about what you have using 'tener'.

sentences.english: No, I don't have a book.
No, I don't have a house.
How much money do you have?

sentences.spanish: Tengo un amigo en el centro.
Tengo una amiga en la escuela.
sentences.english: I have a friend (female) at school.

sentences.spanish: Ana está en casa y tiene hambre.
sentences.english: Ana is at home and is hungry.

sentences.spanish: Luis está en el café y tiene sed.
sentences.english: Luis is in the café and is thirsty.

title: Chapter 5: Regular -AR Verbs
description: Learn basic actions like speaking, working, and studying.

sentences.english: Today I study and tomorrow I work.

title: Chapter 6: Regular -ER/-IR Verbs
description: Action verbs like eating, drinking, living, and opening.

sentences.spanish: Vivo en la ciudad y trabajo en el centro.
sentences.english: I live in the city and work downtown.

sentences.spanish: Abro el libro y escribo un mensaje.
sentences.english: I open the book and write a message.
I receive a message and respond.

title: Chapter 7: Reflexive Verbs
description: Daily routines and actions done to oneself.

sentences.spanish: Por la mañana me levanto y me ducho.
sentences.english: In the morning I get up and shower.

sentences.spanish: Por la mañana me lavo las manos.
sentences.english: In the morning I wash my hands.

sentences.spanish: Por la mañana me visto y salgo.
sentences.english: In the morning I get dressed and leave.
Today I go to bed early.

sentences.spanish: Me llamo Ana y vivo aquí.
sentences.english: My name is Ana and I live here.

sentences.spanish: Te llamas Luis y trabajas en el centro.
sentences.english: Your name is Luis and you work downtown.

sentences.spanish: Se llama Marta y estudia español.
sentences.english: Her name is Marta and she studies Spanish.

sentences.spanish: Se llama Carlos y compra café.
sentences.english: His name is Carlos and he buys coffee.
Do you shower in the morning?

sentences.spanish: Sí, me ducho por la mañana.
sentences.english: Yes, I shower in the morning.

sentences.spanish: No, no me ducho por la mañana.
sentences.english: No, I don't shower in the morning.

sentences.spanish: Nos levantamos, nos vestimos y salimos.
sentences.english: We get up, get dressed, and leave.

title: Chapter 8: Expressing Likes
description: Use 'gustar' to express what you like.

sentences.spanish: No, no me gusta el té.
No, no me gustan los libros.
A Ana le gusta la música.
A Luis le gusta el fútbol.
Me gusta el café y el té.
Me gusta el café, pero no me gusta el té.
sentences.english: I like coffee, but I don't like tea.

sentences.spanish: Me gustan los libros, pero no me gustan las fotos.
sentences.english: I like books, but I don't like photos.

sentences.spanish: A mí me gusta el café.
A ti te gusta el café.
Me gusta estar en el café.
sentences.english: I like being in the café.

sentences.spanish: Sí, me gusta trabajar en el centro.
No, no me gusta trabajar en el centro.
sentences.english: No, I don't like working downtown.

sentences.spanish: A Ana le gustan los libros.
A Luis le gustan las fotos.
A Marta le gusta el café.
A Carlos le gusta la sopa.
Me gusta estudiar por la mañana.
sentences.english: I like studying in the morning.

title: Chapter 9: The Near Future
description: Express what you are going to do using 'ir + a'.

sentences.english: I am going to study today.
You are going to work today.
He/She is going to buy bread.
We are going to eat at home.
They are going to drink coffee.
Are you going to study tomorrow?
Yes, I am going to study tomorrow.

sentences.spanish: No, no voy a estudiar mañana.
sentences.english: No, I am not going to study tomorrow.
I am going to go to the café.

sentences.spanish: Vas a ir a la escuela.
sentences.english: You are going to go to school.
He/She is going to go to work.
We are going to go downtown.
Where are you going to be today?

sentences.spanish: Hoy voy a estar en casa.
sentences.english: Today I'm going to be at home.

sentences.spanish: Hoy voy a estar en el trabajo.
sentences.english: Today I'm going to be at work.
I am going to look for a book.
You are going to wait here.
He/She is going to listen to music.
We are going to speak Spanish.
I am going to open the door.
You are going to write a message.
He/She is going to receive a letter.
Are you going to buy the car?

sentences.spanish: Sí, voy a comprar el coche.
sentences.english: Yes, I'm going to buy the car.

sentences.spanish: No, no voy a comprar el coche.
sentences.english: No, I'm not going to buy the car.

sentences.spanish: Voy a estudiar en el café.
sentences.english: I am going to study in the café.

sentences.spanish: Vamos a estudiar y después vamos a comer.
sentences.english: We are going to study and then we are going to eat.
I am going to call Ana.
You are going to call Luis.
He/She is going to call Marta.
Yes, I am going to come.
No, I am not going to come.
I am going to stay at home.

sentences.spanish: No voy a quedarme en casa.
sentences.english: I am not going to stay at home.

sentences.spanish: ¿Te gusta el café? Sí, me gusta.
sentences.english: Do you like coffee? Yes, I do.

sentences.spanish: Voy a trabajar hoy, pero mañana estudio.
sentences.english: I'm going to work today, but tomorrow I study.

title: Chapter 10: The Preterite Tense
description: Talk about completed past actions.

sentences.english: Yesterday I studied in the café.
Yesterday I looked for a book.
Yesterday I waited in the street.
Yesterday I looked at a photo.
Last night we ate at home.
Yesterday I went to the café.
Did you go to the café yesterday?
Yes, I went to the café yesterday.

sentences.spanish: No, no fui al café ayer.
sentences.english: No, I didn't go to the café yesterday.
No, I didn't buy bread yesterday.
Yesterday I went to bed late.
Yesterday I didn't buy the car.
Yesterday we talked and then we ate.

sentences.spanish: Ayer busqué un café y lo encontré.
sentences.english: Yesterday I looked for a café and found it.

sentences.spanish: Ayer esperé y después fui a casa.
sentences.english: Yesterday I waited and then I went home.

title: Chapter 11: The Imperfect Tense
description: Describe ongoing past actions and habits.

sentences.english: Before, I used to study at home.
Before, I used to work downtown.
Before, I used to live here.
When I was a student, I studied a lot.
When I was young, I worked little.

sentences.spanish: Siempre comía pan por la mañana.
sentences.english: I always ate bread in the morning.

sentences.spanish: Todos los días me levantaba temprano.
sentences.english: Every day I got up early.

sentences.spanish: Todas las noches me acostaba tarde.
sentences.english: Every night I went to bed late.
In the morning I used to shower.

sentences.spanish: Ayer Ana estaba en el café.
sentences.english: Yesterday Ana was in the café.

sentences.spanish: Mientras yo estudiaba, él escuchaba música.
sentences.english: While I was studying, he was listening to music.
While you were working, I was waiting.
While we were eating, we were talking.
Yesterday I was studying when you called me.

sentences.spanish: Ayer escuchaba música cuando abriste la puerta.
sentences.english: Yesterday I was listening to music when you opened the door.
Before, I used to sit here.
Before, I used to stay at home.
Did you use to work here before?
Yes, I used to work here before.
No, I didn't use to work here before.

sentences.spanish: Antes hablaba poco, ahora hablo más.
sentences.english: Before I spoke little; now I speak more.

sentences.spanish: Antes vivía lejos, ahora vivo cerca.
sentences.english: Before I lived far; now I live near.
When I was a student, I didn't work.

sentences.spanish: Cuando era estudiante, me gustaba el café.
sentences.english: When I was a student, I liked coffee.

title: Chapter 12: The Present Perfect
description: Talk about what you 'have done'.

sentences.english: I have looked for a job.
You have looked for a café.
He/She has looked for a house.
This week I have studied a lot.
This week I have worked a lot.
Have you gone to the café today?

sentences.spanish: Sí, he ido al café hoy.
sentences.english: Yes, I have gone to the café today.

sentences.spanish: No, no he ido al café hoy.
sentences.english: No, I haven't gone to the café today.
Yesterday I studied; today I have studied.
Before I used to study; today I have studied.

title: Chapter 13: Direct Object Pronouns
description: Replace nouns with 'lo, la, los, las'.

sentences.spanish: ¿Dónde está el libro? No lo veo.
sentences.english: Where is the book? I don’t see it.

sentences.spanish: ¿Dónde está la llave? No la encuentro.
sentences.english: Where is the key? I can’t find it.

title: Chapter 14: Indirect Object Pronouns
description: Express 'to whom' or 'for whom' using 'le' and 'les'.

sentences.english: I give the book to Ana.
I give the letter to Luis.
Do you give money to your brother?
Do you give time to your friend?
No, I don’t give her time.
We give the bread to them.
We give the soup to you (pl.).

sentences.spanish: Hoy le he escrito a Marta.
Hoy le he llamado a Carlos.
Le gusta el café a Ana.
A Ana le gusta el café.

sentences.english: I give him/her the book now.

sentences.spanish: No le doy el libro ahora.
sentences.english: I don’t give him/her the book now.
Do you talk to your friend?

title: Chapter 15: Double Object Pronouns
description: Use both direct and indirect object pronouns together.

sentences.spanish: Le doy el libro a Ana.
Le doy la carta a Luis.
Les doy los libros a ellos.
Les doy las llaves a ustedes.

sentences.english: Do you give it to him/her now?

sentences.spanish: No, no se lo doy ahora.
sentences.english: No, I don’t give it now.
Do you give it to him/her today?
Yesterday I gave it to him/her.
Yesterday I gave it to him/her.
Today I’ve given it to him/her.
Today I’ve given it to him/her.
I want to give it to him/her.
I want to give it to him/her.
I’m going to give it to him/her.
I’m going to give it to him/her.

title: Chapter 16: Affirmative / Negative Words
description: Learn to use always, never, something, nothing, etc.

title: Chapter 17: Por vs. Para
description: Understand the differences between por and para.

title: Chapter 18: Telling Time & Weather
description: Learn to express hours and weather.

sentences.english: I want you to live here.
I want you to study today.
I want you to work tomorrow.
I want you to open the door.
I want you to close the window.
I want you to wait here.
I want you to listen to me.
I want you to look at me.

sentences.spanish: Quiero que me digas la verdad.
sentences.english: I want you to tell me the truth.
I want you to tell it to me.

sentences.spanish: Quiero que se lo des a Ana.
sentences.english: I want you to give it to Ana.

sentences.spanish: Quiero que se la des a Luis.
sentences.english: I want you to give it to Luis.
I want you to come now.
I want you to go with me.
Do you want me to speak?
Yes, I want you to speak.
No, I don't want you to speak.
Do you want me to open the door?

sentences.spanish: Sí, quiero que abras la puerta.
sentences.english: Yes, I want you to open the door.
I want you to arrive early.
I want you not to arrive late.
I want you to sit here.

sentences.spanish: Quiero que te quedes en casa.
sentences.english: I want you to stay at home.
I want you to buy bread.
I want you to buy coffee.
I want you to buy it today.
I want you to buy it today.
I want you to buy them today.

title: Chapter 19: Imperative Mood
description: Give commands and instructions.

sentences.english: I hope you live well here.
I hope you open the door.
I hope you close the window.
I hope you listen to me.

sentences.spanish: Espero que me digas la verdad.
sentences.english: I hope you tell me the truth.
I hope you tell it to me.
I hope you go with me.
Do you hope that I speak?
No, I don't expect you to speak.
I hope you don't arrive late.
I hope you buy it today.
I hope you buy it today.
I hope you buy them today.
I hope you buy them today.

title: Chapter 20: Comparisons
description: Compare things using 'más que', 'menos que', and 'tan como'.

sentences.english: It's important that you eat well.
It's important that you drink water.
It's important that you sleep well.
It's important that you arrive early.

sentences.spanish: Es importante que no llegues tarde.
sentences.english: It's important that you not arrive late.
It's important that you come tomorrow.
It's important that you go today.

sentences.spanish: Es importante que abras la puerta.
sentences.english: It's important that you open the door.

sentences.spanish: Es importante que cierres la ventana.
sentences.english: It's important that you close the window.
It's important that you listen to me.

sentences.spanish: Es importante que me digas la verdad.
sentences.english: It's important that you tell me the truth.

sentences.spanish: Es importante que me lo digas.
sentences.english: It's important that you tell it to me.

sentences.spanish: Es importante que se lo des a Ana.
sentences.english: It's important that you give it to Ana.

sentences.spanish: Es importante que se la des a Luis.
sentences.english: It's important that you give it to Luis.
It's important that you have time.
It's important that you have money.
It's important that you are here.
It's important that you are well.
It's important that you are patient.
It's important that you are kind.

sentences.spanish: Es importante que lo compres hoy.
sentences.english: It's important that you buy it today.

sentences.spanish: Es importante que la compres hoy.
sentences.english: It's important that you buy it today.

sentences.spanish: Es importante que los compres hoy.
sentences.english: It's important that you buy them today.

sentences.spanish: Es importante que las compres hoy.
sentences.english: It's important that you buy them today.

title: Chapter 21: The Conditional Tense
description: Talk about what 'would' happen.

sentences.english: If you have time, you come.
If you have money, you buy bread.
If you work a lot, you earn money.
If you eat well, you sleep well.
If you drink water, you feel better.
If you arrive early, we talk.

sentences.spanish: Si llegas tarde, no comemos juntos.
sentences.english: If you arrive late, we don't eat together.

sentences.spanish: Si vienes mañana, vamos al café.
sentences.english: If you come tomorrow, we go to the café.

sentences.spanish: Si vas hoy, compras el libro.
sentences.english: If you go today, you buy the book.
If you open the door, he/she comes in.

sentences.spanish: Si cierras la ventana, está mejor.
sentences.english: If you close the window, it's better.
If you listen to me, you understand.

sentences.spanish: Si me dices la verdad, te ayudo.
sentences.english: If you tell me the truth, I help you.

sentences.spanish: Si me lo dices, lo entiendo.
sentences.english: If you tell it to me, I understand it.

sentences.spanish: Si lo compras hoy, es más barato.
sentences.english: If you buy it today, it's cheaper.

sentences.spanish: Si la compras hoy, es más barata.
sentences.english: If you buy it today, it's cheaper.

sentences.spanish: Si los compras hoy, es mejor.
sentences.english: If you buy them today, it's better.

sentences.spanish: Si las compras hoy, es mejor.
sentences.english: If you buy them today, it's better.
If you're hungry, you eat now.
If you're thirsty, you drink water.
If you're here, I see you.

sentences.spanish: Si no estás aquí, no te veo.
sentences.english: If you're not here, I don't see you.
If you want, I open the door.
If you want, I wait for you.
If you can, you come now.
If you can't, you come tomorrow.

sentences.spanish: Si llueve, me quedo en casa.
sentences.english: If it rains, I stay at home.

sentences.spanish: Si hace sol, vamos al centro.
sentences.english: If it's sunny, we go downtown.

title: Chapter 22: The Future Tense
description: Talk about what 'will' happen.

sentences.english: If I had time, I would come.

sentences.spanish: Si tuviera dinero, compraría el libro.
sentences.english: If I had money, I would buy the book.
If you studied more, you would learn more.
If you worked less, you would rest more.
If you ate better, you would sleep better.
If you drank water, you would feel better.
If you arrived early, we would talk more.

sentences.spanish: Si llegaras tarde, no comeríamos juntos.
sentences.english: If you arrived late, we wouldn't eat together.

sentences.spanish: Si vinieras mañana, iríamos al café.
sentences.english: If you came tomorrow, we would go to the café.

sentences.spanish: Si fueras hoy, comprarías el pan.
sentences.english: If you went today, you would buy the bread.
If you opened the door, he/she would come in.

sentences.spanish: Si cerraras la ventana, estaría mejor.
sentences.english: If you closed the window, it would be better.
If you listened to me, you would understand.

sentences.spanish: Si me dijeras la verdad, te ayudaría.
sentences.english: If you told me the truth, I would help you.

sentences.spanish: Si me lo dijeras, lo entendería.
sentences.english: If you told it to me, I would understand it.

sentences.spanish: Si lo compraras hoy, sería más barato.
sentences.english: If you bought it today, it would be cheaper.

sentences.spanish: Si la compraras hoy, sería más barata.
sentences.english: If you bought it today, it would be cheaper.

sentences.spanish: Si los compraras hoy, sería mejor.
sentences.english: If you bought them today, it would be better.

sentences.spanish: Si las compraras hoy, sería mejor.
sentences.english: If you bought them today, it would be better.
If you were here, I would see you.

sentences.spanish: Si no estuvieras aquí, no te vería.
sentences.english: If you weren't here, I wouldn't see you.

sentences.spanish: Si fueras paciente, todo sería más fácil.
sentences.english: If you were patient, everything would be easier.

sentences.spanish: Si fueras amable, él hablaría contigo.
sentences.english: If you were kind, he would speak with you.
If I could, I would go now.
If I couldn't, I would go tomorrow.
If I wanted, I would speak.
If you wanted, I would open the door.

sentences.spanish: Si lloviera, me quedaría en casa.
sentences.english: If it rained, I would stay at home.

sentences.spanish: Si hiciera sol, iríamos al centro.
sentences.english: If it were sunny, we would go downtown.
If you came with me, I would be happy.

title: Chapter 23: The Present Subjunctive
description: Express desires, doubts, and hypotheticals.

sentences.spanish: Acabo de darle el libro a Ana.
sentences.english: I just gave Ana the book.
I just gave it to her.

sentences.spanish: Acabo de darle la carta a Luis.
sentences.english: I just gave Luis the letter.
I just gave it to him.
I just told him/her the truth.

title: Chapter 24: Subjunctive vs. Indicative
description: Practice choosing the correct mood.

sentences.english: I’ve been studying for one hour.
You’ve been studying for one hour.
He/She has been studying for one hour.
We’ve been studying for one hour.
I’ve been working for two hours.
You’ve been working for two hours.
He/She has been working for two hours.
We’ve been working for two hours.
I’ve been waiting for half an hour.
You’ve been waiting for half an hour.
He/She has been waiting for half an hour.
I’ve been living here for a long time.
You’ve been living here for a long time.
He/She has been living here for a long time.
I’ve been talking for twenty minutes.
You’ve been talking for twenty minutes.
He/She has been talking for twenty minutes.

sentences.spanish: Llevo todo el día pensando en eso.
sentences.english: I’ve been thinking about that all day.

sentences.spanish: Llevas todo el día mirando el teléfono.
sentences.english: You’ve been looking at the phone all day.

sentences.spanish: Lleva todo el día escuchando música.
sentences.english: He/She has been listening to music all day.
I’ve been looking for work for one week.

sentences.spanish: Llevas una semana buscando el libro.
sentences.english: You’ve been looking for the book for one week.
He/She has been looking for a house for one week.
I’ve been reading it for one hour.
I’ve been writing it for one hour.
He/She has been giving it to him/her for one hour.
Have you been waiting a long time?
Yes, I’ve been waiting a long time.
Have you been working here a long time?

sentences.spanish: No, no llevo mucho tiempo trabajando aquí.
sentences.english: No, I haven’t been working here long.
I’ve been learning Spanish for years.
